Wonderfully directed by Alyssa Rallo Bennettt, with amazing acting performances by the legendary Christopher Lloyd, as well as an incredibly talented young cast.A must see film for people of all ages! Congratulations to the Bennett's! Christopher Lloyd should be nominated for an Oscar! Gorgeous. Caryn James   The Hollywood Reporter  10/19/2018It seems like an act of immense generosity that Christopher Lloyd has added his warm, affecting presence to ReRun, an otherwise pallid and clumsy contemporary spin on It's a Wonderful Life.  Go to Full Review       Prairie Miller   WBAI Radio  10/11/2018Veteran time traveler on screen Christopher Lloyd - Back To The Future and many more - is at it once again in ReRun. Never disappointing and in this case lending new meaning to the notion of 'in the closet' - though with more metaphysical implications.  Go to Full Review     Read all reviews

At times, I felt as though I was watching an onstage performance rather than a movie, but this independent film kept me intrigued until the very end.   See more          11/02/2018  Review Touching without being cloying or manipulative, this great indie film takes a fresh and honest look at growing old and the choices we make through the eyes of George Benson (played young adeptly by a very charming Teo Rapp-Olson and old by the immensely talented Christopher Lloyd), who is given the chance to relive a critical day in his life thanks to his grandson's magical closet. What makes this movie so great and honest is that instead of it turning into a trope of "look how much better off all these other people are as a result of your actions", George

results.title: Rerun Movie
results.content:
## 

  ReRun is a mind-bending journey that explores the fragile line between memory and reality.
 ## 

  Step into Hughie’s world as he undergoes an experimental therapy that unlocks his deepest memories.
 ## 

  A journey through the past reveals the unsettling truth hidden within an old VHS tape.

## Directoral Debut

“ReRun” marks the directorial debut of Jay Worsley,  who also wrote the film’s screenplay. Jay is an Emmy award-winning Director of Photography with a deep passion for storytelling.

With a background in visual artistry, Jay brings a fresh and unique perspective to his first feature-length film. Writing and directing the film allowed him to bring a deeply personal vision to life, blending psychological intrigue with emotional depth. [...] Skip to Content 

ReRun Movie

MAIN FILM Balanced Copy 01.01_32_02_00.Still065.jpg

[](#lightbox_

# Watch the trailer

Hughie grapples with the complexities of his memories and the emotional weight they carry. When his therapist refers him to an enigmatic program called ‘Re-Run,’ Hughie embarks on a mind-bending journey through his past. As the lines blur between reality and perception, he is forced to confront the haunting truths hidden within his subconscious. ‘ReRun’ explores the fragile nature of memory and how our emotions shape the stories we tell ourselves which in turn, shapes our own character.

Sequence 15.00_00_04_12.Still001.jpg

# THERE ARE TWO SIDES TO EVERY MEMORY

# -

# THERE ARE TWO SIDES TO EVERY MEMORY -

## THE CAST

# RERUN MERCH SHOP [...] Known for his stunning cinematography and attention to detail, “ReRun” is a bold statement as both a writer and director, signaling the arrival of an exciting new voice in cinema.

## DIRECTORS OF PHOTOGRAPHY

Eric Floberg and Steven Schultz serve as the Directors of Photography for “ReRun.” Known for their work on the documentary “Moving Still,” they bring a strong visual style and storytelling expertise to the film.

Their cinematography adds depth and emotion to the story, enhancing the film’s exploration of memory and reality. Their collaboration ensures “ReRun” is visually captivating and immersive.

results.title: Rerun
results.content: It has been common practice by networks, notably the BBC, to rerun some series after they have not fared particularly well on their original run. This was particularly common with sitcoms such as The Office "The Office (British TV series)"), which had very low ratings in its first series, as well as a poor reception from both critics and focus groups and was almost cancelled. The series started to gain traction once the BBC decided to repeat it in a different timeslot and The Office went on to be an award-winning and critically acclaimed show which has regularly featured in lists of the Best Sitcoms ever. In 2019, the series was ranked 6th on The Guardian's list of the 100 best TV shows of the 21st century. [...] Traditionally, shows most likely to be rerun in this manner are scripted comedies and dramas. Such shows are more likely to be considered evergreen content "Evergreen (media)") that can be rerun for a long period of time without losing its cultural relevance. Game shows, variety shows, Saturday-morning cartoons, and to a lesser extent, news magazines, tabloid talk shows, and late-night talk shows (often in edited form) have been seen less commonly in reruns; game shows can quickly become dated because of inflation, while talk shows often draw humor from contemporary events. Tabloid talk shows, with episode archives of thousands of episodes, tend to do well in reruns compared to talk shows without an element of conflict. Newsmagazines, especially those that focus on evergreen stories such [...] especially those that focus on evergreen stories such as true crime, are easily repackaged for the syndication market, which (along with much lower residuals compared to a scripted series with star talent) can also make them cheaper than a first-run program or scripted reruns. Most variants of reality television have proven to be a comparative failure in reruns, due to a number of factors (high cast turnover, loss of the element of surprise, overall hostility toward the format, and lack of media cross-promotion among them); some self-contained and personality-driven reality shows have been successfully rerun. Reruns of sports broadcasts, which face many of the same issues reality shows face, have found a niche on multichannel television and free ad-supported streaming television.
